analysis.period_summary: For March 30 – April 5, AstroLover Sketch generated $6,853 USD net revenue from 117 frontend sales across 6 active funnel flows. The dominant flow /c/ → /offer/v4/ drove 106 purchases but suffers from a critical 6.16% checkout-to-purchase rate, representing the single largest revenue leak in the funnel.

funnel_scorecard.commentary: Funnel health is poor overall. The dominant /c/ → /offer/v4/ flow has strong optin (16.99%) and checkout click-through (83.41%) rates but catastrophically converts only 6.16% of ClickBank checkout clicks to purchases. Backend monetization is weak with OTO1 at 30.8% (below 40% strong threshold) and OTO2 at 12.8% (below 15% minimum threshold).

funnel_leaks.why_it_matters: 1,720 checkout clicks converted only 106 sales; fixing to 20% would add ~238 sales and ~$8,806 revenue at $37 AOV.
Only 57.89% of offer viewers clicked checkout vs 83.41% on v4; improving to 80% would add ~4 checkout clicks and potentially 1-2 sales in this smaller flow.
Critically low offer-to-checkout rate suggests /offer/v3 copy or CTA is broken; 5 checkout clicks yielded 0 sales, indicating dual failure points.
Landing page /a converts at 2.27% vs 16.99% benchmark, losing 6-7 potential optins from 44 visitors; likely broken form or severe traffic-message mismatch.

upsell_analysis.commentary: Backend monetization is weak. OTO1 Blueprint at 30.8% take rate is below the 40%+ strong threshold, and OTO2 Timeline at 12.8% is critically below the 15% minimum, suggesting poor offer positioning or broken redirect logic. Average revenue per buyer of $58.57 includes backend, but OTO performance leaves significant money on the table.

needle_movers.why: 1,720 checkout clicks converted only 106 purchases (6.16%) on the dominant flow, far below the 20-30% target. This indicates ClickBank payment page friction, price shock, or technical abandonment issues.
what: Audit ClickBank checkout flow: confirm payment page is loading correctly, no unexpected fees shown, and mobile experience is functional.
A/B test adding a 'What happens next' trust section immediately before the checkout CTA on /offer/v4/ to reduce sticker shock.
Implement exit-intent popup on checkout page offering $10 discount code to recapture abandoners (test with ClickBank support to ensure compliance).
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Improving checkout-to-purchase from 6.16% to 20% would add ~238 sales/week × $37 = $8,806 frontend revenue, plus ~$4,400 backend = $13,206 total weekly lift.

needle_movers.why: OTO2 Timeline converts at 12.8%, critically below the 15% minimum threshold, with only 15 sales from 117 frontend buyers. Downsell at 1.7% suggests the offer sequence or redirect logic may be broken.
what: Verify OTO2 redirect fires correctly after OTO1 (accept or decline) — audit ClickBank Instant Notification postback URLs and test funnel end-to-end.
Rewrite OTO2 VSL/copy to emphasize scarcity and consequence of missing the Timeline reading (current copy likely fails to differentiate from Blueprint).
Test swapping OTO2 full price to $37 and downsell to $27 to reduce friction and increase volume (measure total revenue impact, not just take rate).
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Improving OTO2 from 12.8% to 25% would add ~14 sales/week × $47 average = $658 weekly backend lift; fixing broken redirects could unlock the missing 80+ OTO2 impressions entirely.

needle_movers.why: OTO1 Blueprint at 30.8% is below the 40%+ strong threshold, leaving 10-15 sales per week on the table. With 117 buyers, a 10-point lift would add ~12 OTO1 sales worth ~$804/week.
what: Test reordering OTO1 copy: lead with the 'missing ingredient' angle (what the frontend sketch cannot reveal without the Blueprint) rather than feature list.
Add video testimonial showing a customer using Blueprint to unlock deeper insight (social proof at decision moment).
A/B test OTO1 price drop to $57 to measure elasticity — volume increase may offset margin compression.
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Lifting OTO1 from 30.8% to 40% would add ~11 sales/week × $67 = $737 weekly; combined OTO1+OTO2 fix could add $1,395/week backend revenue.

needle_movers.why: Three low-volume flows (campaigns 91, 95, 98) generated 130 total views, 20 optins, 10 checkout clicks, and 0 sales. These are diluting reporting, wasting analytical focus, and may indicate broken tracking or abandoned tests.
what: Pause campaigns 91, 95, 98 in CPV Labs and redirect remnant email traffic to the proven /c/ → /offer/v4/ flow.
Archive /a, /offer, and /offer/v3 page variants to eliminate confusion in analytics and focus optimization efforts on the two working flows.
Document learnings from /offer/v3 (35.71% offer-to-checkout suggests weak CTA) before archiving to inform future v4 improvements.
needle_movers.revenue_impact: Cleanup has no direct revenue impact but frees analytical bandwidth to focus on the 99%+ revenue-generating flows; stopping broken tests prevents future wasted ad spend.

needle_movers.why: This smaller flow (292 views, 14 purchases) achieves a remarkable 41.18% checkout-to-purchase rate, 6.7× better than the dominant c→offer_v4 flow. While sample size is small (34 checkout clicks), the delta is large enough to warrant investigation.
what: Identify traffic source difference: compare campaign 100 (email split test B/C) vs campaign 101 (organic + main email) to isolate what makes /b /c/ traffic convert better at checkout.
Hypothesis: /b or /c/ landing page may pre-qualify buyers better or set different price expectations — audit copy differences and consider applying winning elements to main /c/ page.
Do NOT scale this flow yet (insufficient sample size) but monitor weekly; if checkout-to-purchase holds >35% over 100+ clicks, consider shifting traffic allocation.
needle_movers.revenue_impact: If /b /c/ checkout conversion pattern (41.18%) could be replicated on the main flow's 1,720 weekly checkout clicks, potential gain is ~602 sales vs current 106 = +$18,352 weekly revenue (hypothesis only, requires validation).

cpv_cb_discrepancy.note: CPV tracked 123 conversions vs ClickBank 117 frontend sales; 6-conversion delta is within expected range as CPV includes OTO postbacks, and revenue difference of $111 (1.6%) is negligible after SGD/USD conversion.

analysis.data_notes: GA4 supplement is empty (has_custom_events=false), so no optin_page_views data available to measure form-page-to-submit drop-off. Three test flows (landing→offer, landing→offer_v3, a→offer_v2) have insufficient sample size (<20 checkout clicks each) and generated zero sales, suggesting either broken tracking or abandoned split tests that should be cleaned up. The /b /c/ → /offer/v4/ flow shows exceptionally strong 41.18% checkout-to-purchase rate but on only 34 clicks, requiring continued monitoring before drawing firm conclusions.
